title: Bender Gets Made

jokes.text: No, this one is! Wait, that one was worse. Ah, so far so good on this one!
jokes.setup: Fry declares each second is the greatest of his life
jokes.punchline: His immediate self-correction and real-time evaluation of each moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - his childlike enthusiasm combined with overthinking creates genuine character comedy

jokes.text: Cooking Slugs For Dinner and Cooking Dinner for Slugs
jokes.setup: Listing Elzar's cookbooks
jokes.punchline: The reversal creates two very different scenarios - cooking slugs vs. cooking for slug customers
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - the reversal creates genuinely different concepts

jokes.text: Now, this is why I prefer your non-stick robots.
jokes.setup: Bender gets injured by hot spice
jokes.punchline: Elzar casually treats robots as kitchen equipment rather than people
jokes.explanation: Dark but fitting for Futurama's robot prejudice theme - Elzar's casual dehumanization is appropriately uncomfortable

jokes.text: Step one: Boil some oysters in a pot of Neptune-style gumbo.
jokes.setup: Teaching how to make gumbo
jokes.punchline: Step one is to add oysters to already-made gumbo - circular logic
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever observation about TV cooking shows' illogical instruction order

jokes.text: Bender, try to be a little quieter. - No, you shut up!
jokes.setup: Fry tries to calm down the disruptive Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate hostile rejection of any social correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his reflexive rudeness and inability to be embarrassed

jokes.text: Now and then, knock it up a notch with your spice weasel.
jokes.setup: Parody of Emeril's 'kick it up a notch' catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Using a live 'spice weasel' instead of spices
jokes.explanation: Creative twist on a familiar cooking show trope - spice weasel became an iconic Futurama element

jokes.text: Bam! Yeah! Bam it again! Knock it up a notch! Who let him into the studio? Come on! Work that weasel! Quit holding out!
jokes.setup: Bender getting overly excited about the cooking demonstration
jokes.punchline: His increasingly inappropriate and sexual-sounding demands escalate the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Bender's complete lack of social awareness and inappropriate enthusiasm

jokes.text: My eye! I'm blind!
jokes.setup: Elzar throws spice at the camera/audience
jokes.punchline: Leela, who only has one eye, gets hit and declares blindness
jokes.explanation: Works specifically because Leela only has one eye - character-specific physical comedy

jokes.text: Tell me, doctor, how's my cyst? Grande.
jokes.setup: Patient asking about medical condition
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg answers with coffee size terminology
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that fits Zoidberg's incompetence as a doctor

jokes.text: I don't like this doctor. I've lost more patients than he's treated.
jokes.setup: Expressing distrust of the human doctor
jokes.punchline: The implication that she has killed more patients than he has helped
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good structure, though not particularly character-specific

jokes.text: A grayish blob? Right. And this one? A grayish blob? Not as right that time.
jokes.setup: Doctor doing vision test with Leela
jokes.punchline: Her answers are identical but somehow one is more wrong than the other
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical logic - identical answers somehow have different levels of correctness

jokes.text: but I have more skill in my claw than you have in your whole body!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg challenging the competent human doctor
jokes.punchline: His boast about having skill in his claw when he's completely incompetent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg delusion - his complete lack of self-awareness about his incompetence

jokes.text: Sure. Butter me up with candy. Well, it won't work, sonny!
jokes.setup: Doctor offers Zoidberg medicine/candy
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg accepts while claiming he won't be influenced
jokes.explanation: Classic contradiction humor - saying one thing while doing another

jokes.text: Clack.
jokes.setup: Asked for medical opinion about wearing an eye patch
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's response is just a claw sound effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that perfectly captures Zoidberg's alien nature and medical incompetence

jokes.text: Leela! We're right here! I'm not deaf! I just have to wear a patch. Does it look stupid? No! It looks so nice, I think I might get one too.
jokes.setup: Fry treats blind Leela like she can't hear either
jokes.punchline: Her irritated correction followed by his sweet but absurd compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's well-meaning but misguided attempts to help

jokes.text: It's Elzar! I'm so excited I wish I could wet my pants!
jokes.setup: Bender expressing extreme excitement about seeing Elzar
jokes.punchline: Robots can't wet themselves, making his wish both crude and impossible
jokes.explanation: Clever robot-specific joke - Bender wanting to experience a human expression of excitement he can't actually have

jokes.text: Hooray for blind-ey! Oh, God. I'm coming down!
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrating getting invited to dinner
jokes.punchline: Bender's cruel nickname for Leela followed by apparent drug crash
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate - Bender's casual cruelty and substance issues

jokes.text: Jerk chicken, jerk beef, jerk pork! Is there any meat he can't jerk?
jokes.setup: Fry listing the jerk-seasoned meats on his plate
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'jerk' as seasoning vs. the verb
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual double entendre - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: The king crab is to die for! Look, a tiny, edible crown! What's it made of? Wood.
jokes.setup: Amy praising the fancy king crab with its decorative crown
jokes.punchline: The 'edible' crown is made of inedible wood
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up fancy restaurant expectations then undercuts with absurd reality

jokes.text: It's robot food. It's so good! Just try a little. No, really, I don't... Are you all right? Oh, yeah, the salad's fantastic! So fresh.
jokes.setup: Amy insisting Fry try robot food despite his protests
jokes.punchline: Fry's obvious poisoning reaction that he tries to cover up politely
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy - person tries food, gets sick, pretends to be fine

jokes.text: Oh, my! That steamed carrot was a bit spicy.
jokes.setup: Presumably the Professor had an extreme reaction to food
jokes.punchline: His massive understatement calling it 'a bit spicy'
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor fitting the Professor's academic demeanor

jokes.text: Hey! A bill? You're charging us, after you blinded me? I made a nice meal. This ain't a charity. Twelve hundred dollars? Holy zombie Jesus!
jokes.setup: The group discovers they're being charged for the 'apology' dinner
jokes.punchline: The outrageous price and Bender's bizarre exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with memorable Futurama-specific exclamation - 'Holy zombie Jesus' became quotable

jokes.text: We don't have that! Especially not him. They took away my credit cards.
jokes.setup: Leela saying they don't have money
jokes.punchline: Singling out Bender as especially broke, followed by his admission about credit cards
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor about Bender's financial irresponsibility

jokes.text: I was picking my nose. Picking his nose! Get him!
jokes.setup: Bender explains his hand movement to his face
jokes.punchline: Cop misinterprets this innocent action as threatening
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - innocent explanation gets twisted into criminal activity

jokes.text: Don't hit me! I'm brittle!
jokes.setup: Bender facing police brutality
jokes.punchline: Claims physical vulnerability despite being made of metal
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - cowardice overriding his robot durability logic

jokes.text: I don't know. I try to hire people who are less unbearable.
jokes.setup: Asked if he'll let Bender work off the debt
jokes.punchline: His honest assessment that he prefers less unbearable employees
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan honesty - Elzar's blunt assessment delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Bender's a model employee! He's so polite! And hardworking. He's made of candy.
jokes.setup: Friends trying to vouch for Bender's character
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and false claims, ending with nonsensical candy assertion
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from plausible lies to complete absurdity - the candy claim is perfectly random

jokes.text: You better keep your nose clean. That'll be easy. I never wear it.
jokes.setup: Cop gives standard warning about staying out of trouble
jokes.punchline: Bender takes the idiom literally since robots don't have permanent noses
jokes.explanation: Excellent robot-specific wordplay - takes human idiom and makes it literally true for robot anatomy

jokes.text: My walk took a little longer than usual. Nibbler took me through the zoo.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining why she's late
jokes.punchline: The implication that her pet led her to the zoo, treating her like a zoo animal
jokes.explanation: Clever implication - suggests Nibbler's secret intelligence by having him lead the blind cyclops to see other unusual creatures

jokes.text: You look beautiful! My favorite artist is Picasso!
jokes.setup: Fry complimenting Leela's makeup
jokes.punchline: His artistic reference implies her makeup looks like abstract cubist art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual joke - implies her makeup is horribly distorted while Fry tries to be supportive with art reference

jokes.text: A gamma-powered mechanical monster with freeway on-ramps for arms, and a coal-black heart!
jokes.setup: Professor offering to build a replacement for Leela
jokes.punchline: Completely over-the-top mechanical monster description for simple delivery job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - unnecessarily complex and destructive solution to simple problem

jokes.text: Bam! Bam yourself.
jokes.setup: Bender enthusiastically uses Elzar's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Elzar's tired, dismissive response to his own catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the spice weasel scene - shows Elzar's annoyance with his own brand

jokes.text: Are they the Robot Mafia? Yes. You call this a table? You call this a table? I wouldn't hit a guy with this!
jokes.setup: Bender asking about dangerous customers, Elzar confirming
jokes.punchline: Mafia's complaint about table quality is that it's not good enough for violence
jokes.explanation: Great character introduction - establishes Robot Mafia's criminal mindset through furniture complaints

jokes.text: I like this guy's lack of style.
jokes.setup: Bender accidentally destroys table while trying to help
jokes.punchline: Don-Bot complimenting Bender's clumsiness as a positive trait
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion - what would normally be criticism becomes praise in criminal context

jokes.text: I give the guy cement shoes which he likes better than his lead ones.
jokes.setup: Don-Bot explaining cement shoes punishment
jokes.punchline: Victim actually prefers them as an upgrade from heavier lead shoes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of classic mafia trope - cement shoes become a customer service upgrade

jokes.text: I'm trying, but it's not easy.
jokes.setup: Accused of stealing from the Don-Bot
jokes.punchline: Bender admits he's attempting theft but finding it difficult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender honesty - admits to attempted crime with characteristic directness

jokes.text: It's a twenty. It is? Oh, I'm sorry, I meant to... Wait a minute!
jokes.setup: Money confusion between Don-Bot and Bender
jokes.punchline: Don-Bot realizes he's being corrected about his own money
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the absurdity of Bender correcting a crime boss about money amounts

jokes.text: I've always wanted to be a goon!
jokes.setup: Don-Bot offering Bender job as hired goon
jokes.punchline: Bender's genuine excitement about criminal career
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - enthusiasm for wrong things delivered with childlike joy

jokes.text: I've always wanted to be a goon! Hey, Elzar! I quit! Okay!
jokes.setup: Don-Bot offers Bender a job as hired muscle
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate enthusiasm and Elzar's indifferent acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender's excitement about crime and Elzar's relief to be rid of him

jokes.text: Wow! You guys run numbers? Ones and zeroes mostly.
jokes.setup: Bender asking about their numbers running operation
jokes.punchline: Don-Bot's literal interpretation since they're computers
jokes.explanation: Excellent robot-specific wordplay - gambling 'numbers' becomes literal binary code

jokes.text: That's what my mom said.
jokes.setup: Warned to watch out for cops
jokes.punchline: Bender's inappropriate 'that's what she said' style response about police advice
jokes.explanation: Weak - generic inappropriate response that doesn't really make sense in context

jokes.text: I'm programmed to only sell oil-aide and write cute backwards letters.
jokes.setup: Robot vendor being complimented on potential for crime
jokes.punchline: His specific and innocent programming limitations
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor - very specific robot programming that's both innocent and oddly specific

jokes.text: Which I'm sort of in.
jokes.setup: Kid robot complimenting the Robot Mafia
jokes.punchline: Bender's modest acknowledgment of his criminal association
jokes.explanation: Good Bender false modesty about criminal career

jokes.text: But check out today's 'Marmaduke.' Solid.
jokes.setup: Cop reporting no suspicious activity
jokes.punchline: His distraction with and approval of a comic strip
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about cops being distracted by mundane things during investigations

jokes.text: Smells nice too. Better than me? Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Cop commenting that Bender smells nice after search
jokes.punchline: Other cop's immediate honest agreement that robot smells better than him
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - cop's immediate self-aware agreement about his own hygiene

jokes.text: File not found.
jokes.setup: Asked to look into his hard drive for mercy file
jokes.punchline: Computer error message indicating he has no mercy
jokes.explanation: Excellent robot-specific humor - computer terminology perfectly expresses complete lack of mercy

jokes.text: That's my name, you numbskull! Don't make me clamp you one!
jokes.setup: Someone suggests 'Clamps' as nickname for Bender
jokes.punchline: Clamps' violent objection to sharing his signature name/method
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Clamps' territorial protection of his gimmick and signature threat

jokes.text: How's Blotto? It's gritty, and I can keep my monogrammed slippers.
jokes.setup: Asking about the nickname 'Blotto'
jokes.punchline: Bender's practical consideration about keeping his personalized footwear
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender's vanity and practical concerns even when joining organized crime

jokes.text: It'll be clamp this, clamp that, ba-da-clamp!
jokes.setup: Describing the upcoming violent job
jokes.punchline: Clamps' musical enthusiasm about his signature clamping method
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor - Clamps turning violence into a musical number shows his obsession

jokes.text: I can't move. Oh, I think I'm gonna output.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be sick to create alibi
jokes.punchline: Using computer term 'output' instead of biological 'throw up'
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific language adaptation - computer terminology for biological function

jokes.text: That's coming out of your pay!
jokes.setup: Leela crashes the ship due to her blindness
jokes.punchline: Her immediate concern about salary deductions despite being unable to see
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Leela's workplace focus even in crisis situation

jokes.text: The secret of robot cooking: Start with a high-quality oil. Then eat it.
jokes.setup: Bender sharing cooking wisdom
jokes.punchline: The 'cooking' is just consuming the ingredient directly
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific humor - subverts cooking expectations with robot dietary needs

jokes.text: I can stink up a hospital with one of those!
jokes.setup: Learning about high-quality cigars they're stealing
jokes.punchline: Bender's specific goal of creating maximum offensive smell in medical setting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender antisocial behavior - his joy at causing maximum public disruption

jokes.text: All without any killing. You've got some fresh new ideas! I admire that.
jokes.setup: Don-Bot explaining their non-violent plan
jokes.punchline: Crime boss treating avoiding murder as innovative thinking
jokes.explanation: Dark humor subverting crime boss expectations - not killing is treated as creative strategy

jokes.text: They won't know what clamped them!
jokes.setup: Clamps boasting about their advantage
jokes.punchline: Adaptation of 'what hit them' using his signature clamping method
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific wordplay - Clamps personalizing common expression with his gimmick

jokes.text: Eyes are for chumps.
jokes.setup: Leela successfully evading missile while blind
jokes.punchline: Her dismissal of vision despite having only one eye normally
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific irony - one-eyed character dismissing vision after successful blind piloting

jokes.text: Wait, the letters that normally say 'Master Steering Wheel Co.' say 'Nibbler.' Oh, no!
jokes.setup: Fry reading something during the chase
jokes.punchline: Discovery that Nibbler is somehow involved in their predicament
jokes.explanation: Plot advancement rather than pure comedy - sets up Nibbler's secret involvement

jokes.text: Great. The one time I forgot to buy flight insurance!
jokes.setup: Ship being shot down during dangerous mission
jokes.punchline: Fry's mundane insurance concerns during life-threatening situation
jokes.explanation: Standard observational humor about mundane concerns during crisis

jokes.text: Think I should use these clamps that I use every day, at every opportunity? You're a genius, you idiot!
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Clamps should use his clamps
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic response highlighting his obvious obsession, ending with contradictory insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Clamps moment - his frustration at obvious suggestions about his signature gimmick

jokes.text: Sorry I'm tardy, old bean. I was enjoying a watercress sandwich.
jokes.setup: Bender arriving late to criminal operation
jokes.punchline: His posh accent and extremely refined excuse for tardiness
jokes.explanation: Good character transformation - Bender adopting exaggerated British upper-class persona

jokes.text: I say, get the hell off me!
jokes.setup: Bender maintaining his posh persona
jokes.punchline: Polite British phrasing combined with crude American expression
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition - refined British introduction with crude American conclusion

jokes.text: Chew on this! What ho! Stop it! Take that, you twit! No, stop it!
jokes.setup: Bender attacking his friends while maintaining British persona
jokes.punchline: Mix of British exclamations with violence, and Fry's ineffective protests
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with character consistency - Bender's fake British persona during violence

jokes.text: Let me take care of the dirty work. Hey, I like your attitude. And your accent. Keep it up and I might get you your own pair of clamps.
jokes.setup: Bender volunteering for violent work
jokes.punchline: Don-Bot offering clamps as reward, threatening Clamps' uniqueness
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Clamps' jealousy - threatens his unique role in the organization

jokes.text: He's gonna get clamps? Clamps! Clamps, clamps, clamps.
jokes.setup: Learning Bender might get his own clamps
jokes.punchline: Clamps' increasingly frantic repetition of his signature word
jokes.explanation: Perfect character breakdown - Clamps losing composure over his trademark being shared

jokes.text: He was like a computer that ran on magic.
jokes.setup: Amy eulogizing the apparently dead Bender
jokes.punchline: Her poetic but technically confused description of a robot
jokes.explanation: Beautiful poetic description that perfectly captures both Bender's nature and Amy's perspective

jokes.text: Yes, I am! Having just regained consciousness!
jokes.setup: Friends expressing relief that Bender is okay
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific and suspicious timing explanation
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection - Bender's overly specific alibi draws attention to his deception

jokes.text: Where am I now?
jokes.setup: Asked where he was last night
jokes.punchline: Professor's confusion about his current location, let alone past locations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor senility moment - can't even establish present location, let alone past

jokes.text: Oh, sweet legal tender!
jokes.setup: Professor receiving cut from criminal proceeds
jokes.punchline: Formal economic term used as term of endearment for money
jokes.explanation: Good Professor vocabulary - academic terminology for simple greed

jokes.text: Maybe someday I'll tell. And maybe someday I won't listen.
jokes.setup: Fry claiming he might someday reveal what happened
jokes.punchline: Leela's preemptive refusal to hear his story
jokes.explanation: Good character relationship moment - Leela's familiar exasperation with Fry's storytelling

tmdb_overview: Bender takes a new job in the Robot Mafia, but his loyalty is tested when he goes along on the robotic gangster's efforts to rob the Planet Express ship.
